root_cause: Pod cannot be scheduled because PodDisruptionBudgets prevent preemption
evidence: Scheduler messages explicitly indicate PodDisruptionBudget-protected victims
Preemption attempts fail due to policy restrictions
Dominant scheduler message: 0/3 nodes are available: 3 Insufficient cpu. preemption: 0/3 nodes are available: 3 cannot evict pod as it would violate the PodDisruptionBudget.

causes.message: PodDisruptionBudget constraints prevent eviction of existing Pods
Scheduler cannot preempt Pods because doing so would violate a PodDisruptionBudget
Scheduler repeatedly retries placement but remains blocked by PDB protections
Pod remains unscheduled due to PodDisruptionBudget constraints

pod:pdb-unschedulable-pod: Scheduling is blocked because preemption would violate a PodDisruptionBudget

likely_causes: PodDisruptionBudget is too restrictive for current replica count
All feasible victim Pods are protected by a PodDisruptionBudget
Cluster capacity is tight enough that placement depends on evicting protected workloads
suggested_checks: Inspect replica counts of workloads protected by the PDB
Consider whether the PDB is stricter than current cluster capacity can support
